what: vitopack — návod od nuly do CRAN
title: vitopack — návod od nuly do CRAN
text: Tento dokument tě provede: 1. instalací nástrojů, 2. vygenerováním dokumentace buildu, 3. spuštěním testů R CMD check, 4. publikací na GitHub nasazením pkgdown stránky, 5. odesláním balíčku na CRAN. Poznámka: balíček byl vygenerován mimo R (v Cowork mode). Než ho poprvé buildneš, musíš spustit devtools::document() — tím roxygen2 přepíše/dovytvoří man/*.Rd z anotací uvnitř R/*.R.

title: vitopack — návod od nuly do CRAN
text: V R konzoli (Windows: RGui nebo RStudio): Ujisti se, že máš: - R 4.1+ (kvůli nativní pipe |>). - Rtools (Windows) — https://cran.r-project.org/bin/windows/Rtools/ - MikTeX nebo TinyTeX pro PDF manuál: r   install.packages("tinytex"); tinytex::install_tinytex()

title: vitopack — návod od nuly do CRAN
text: V RStudiu: File → New Project → Existing Directory → Desktop/vitopack/vitopack Nebo z konzole:

title: vitopack — návod od nuly do CRAN
text: Tím se z roxygen2 komentářů v R/*.R vytvoří soubory v man/ doplní/aktualizuje NAMESPACE.

title: vitopack — návod od nuly do CRAN
text: Pokrytí:

title: vitopack — návod od nuly do CRAN
text: Cíl: 0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 notes.
code: devtools::check() # nebo s manuálem a externími kontrolami devtools::check(manual = TRUE, remote = TRUE)

title: vitopack — návod od nuly do CRAN
text: Čistý PDF se všemi exportovanými funkcemi: Nebo přes terminál:

title: vitopack — návod od nuly do CRAN
text: Pro instalaci lokálně:

title: vitopack — návod od nuly do CRAN
text: Vytvoř repo na GitHubu (např. donvito/vitopack). Pokud používáš jiný název nebo organizaci, uprav URL BugReports v souboru DESCRIPTION _pkgdown.yml — momentálně tam je donvito/vitopack. Inicializuj Git pushni: V repozitáři otevři Settings → Pages nastav: Source: Deploy branch Branch: gh-pages / (root) Po prvním pushi se automaticky spustí GitHub Actions: R-CMD-check — multi-OS check. pkgdown — vygeneruje pushne stránku gh-pages větve. test-coverage — Codecov report. Stránka pak bude na https://donvito.github.io/vitopack/. (Volitelné) lokálně si stránku zkus:
code: cd C:\Users\OndřejVít\Desktop\vitopack\vitopack git init -b main git add . git commit -m "Initial vitopack 0.1.0" git remote add origin https://github.com/donvito/vitopack.git git push -u origin main pkgdown::build_site()

title: vitopack — návod od nuly do CRAN
text: Vyřeš všechny WARNING/NOTE než půjdeš dál.
code: devtools::check_win_devel()       # win-builder (R-devel) devtools::check_win_release()     # win-builder (R-release) rhub::rhub_setup()                # první nastavení rhub::rhub_check()                # check na různých Linux/Win/Mac obrazech

title: vitopack — návod od nuly do CRAN
text: Aktualizuj cran-comments.md reálnými výsledky checků. V DESCRIPTION ověř: Title bez koncového ., Description ve smysluplných větách, Verze ve formátu 0.1.0, Maintainer e-mail aktivní (CRAN posílá potvrzovací mail). Spusť: Skript projde checklistem, vygeneruje tar.gz, nahraje ho přes https://xmpalantir.wu.ac./cransubmit/ pošle e-mail z tvé adresy ondrej.vit@com-pass.cz k potvrzení. CRAN tým odpoví e-mailem (typicky 1–7 dnů). Pokud chce úpravy, uprav, zvedni verzi (např. 0.1.1), zaktualizuj NEWS.md cran-comments.md pošli znovu.

title: vitopack — návod od nuly do CRAN
text: Otaguj release na GitHubu: Vytvoř GitHub release — pkgdown workflow ho zachytí aktualizuje stránku. Pro další verzi:
code: git tag v0.1.0 && git push --tags usethis::use_version("patch")  # 0.1.0 → 0.1.1

title: vitopack — návod od nuly do CRAN
code: vitopack/ ├── DESCRIPTION            # metadata ├── NAMESPACE              # auto-gen z roxygen ├── LICENSE / LICENSE.md   # MIT ├── NEWS.md                # changelog ├── README.md ├── _pkgdown.yml           # konfigurace webu ├── cran-comments.md       # pro CRAN reviewer ├── R/                     # zdrojový kód s roxygen2 komentáři │   ├── vitopack-package.R │   ├── columns.R │   ├── birth-date.R │   ├── colors.R │   ├── io.R │   ├── triangles.R │   └── chain-ladder.R ├── man/                   # auto-gen .Rd (regeneruje document()) ├── tests/testthat/        # testy ├── vignettes/             # úvodní článek └── .github/workflows/     # CI: R-CMD-check, pkgdown, coverage

title: Getting started with vitopack
text: Start long claims data aggregate triangle: Cumulate, read development factors: weighted variant accepts explicit weight matrix (e.g. exposure): visual inspection use create_chl_trg_visualization() (requires plotly package, listed Suggests dependency).
code: df <- data.frame(   origin = c(1, 1, 1, 2, 2, 3),   dev    = c(0, 1, 2, 0, 1, 0),   paid   = c(10, 5, 2, 20, 7, 15) )  trg <- create_triangle(df,                        row_num = "origin",                        col_num = "dev",                        value   = "paid") trg #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] #> [1,]   10    5    2 #> [2,]   20    7   NA #> [3,]   15   NA   NA cum <- create_cumulative_triangle(trg) create_chl_coefs(cum, chl_length = c("full", 2)) #>          CH_L_lengths  0   1        2 #> 1 chain_ladder - full NA 1.4 1.133333 #> 2    chain_ladder - 2 NA 1.4 1.133333 weight <- matrix(1, nrow = nrow(cum), ncol = ncol(cum)) weight[is.na(cum)] <- NA create_chl_coefs_weighted(cum, weight, chl_length = "full") #>          CH_L_lengths  0     1        2 #> 1 chain_ladder - full NA 1.425 1.133333

title: Getting started with vitopack
text: Turn policy table one column per accounting period:
code: policies <- data.frame(   policy_start = as.Date(c("2024-01-15", "2024-06-01")),   policy_end   = as.Date(c("2024-12-31", "2025-05-31")) )  create_policy_exposure_columns_m(   data             = policies,   exp_names        = c("exp_q1", "exp_q2", "exp_q3", "exp_q4"),   start_months     = c("2024-01-01", "2024-04-01", "2024-07-01", "2024-10-01"),   end_months       = c("2024-03-31", "2024-06-30", "2024-09-30", "2024-12-31"),   start_policy_var = "policy_start",   end_policy_var   = "policy_end" )

title: vitopack — actuarial helpers in R
text: vitopack small toolbox utilities grew day--day non-life actuarial work Com-PASS Advisory s.r.o. collects: Builders transformations chain-ladder development triangles (cumulative, decumulative, run-, development factors optional weighting, annual aggregation, plotly visualisation). Helpers turn long policy data per-period exposure columns (create_policy_exposure_columns_m(), create_policy_exposure_days_columns()). Czech birth-number (rodné číslo) parsers. Smooth RGB rainbow palette generators tuned stacked bars. Convenience /O wrappers multi-sheet .xlsx .xlsb files.

title: vitopack — actuarial helpers in R
text: development version GitHub: CRAN:

title: vitopack — actuarial helpers in R
code: library(vitopack)  # Long-form claims data → triangle df <- data.frame(   origin = c(1, 1, 1, 2, 2, 3),   dev    = c(0, 1, 2, 0, 1, 0),   paid   = c(10, 5, 2, 20, 7, 15) ) trg <- create_triangle(df, row_num = "origin", col_num = "dev", value = "paid") cum <- create_cumulative_triangle(trg) create_chl_coefs(cum, chl_length = c("full", 2))  # Czech birth number → date rc_to_birth_day("900615/1234")  # 1990-06-15  # Smooth color palette palette <- get_colors_duo(rgb_colors_for_plot(), 12)

title: vitopack — actuarial helpers in R
text: Issues pull requests welcome. Please run devtools::check() devtools::test() opening PR.

what: Re-aggregate a per-period triangle to an annual triangle — create_annual_triangle
title: Re-aggregate a per-period triangle to an annual triangle — create_annual_triangle
text: Combines period consecutive sub-period diagonals/columns cumulative triangle one annual diagonal/column. Useful source data monthly quarterly report wants annual figures.

title: Re-aggregate a per-period triangle to an annual triangle — create_annual_triangle
Re-aggregate a per-period triangle to an annual triangle — create_annual_triangle
text: cum_trg cumulative numeric triangle. period Integer - number sub-periods make one year (default 4 quarters).

title: Re-aggregate a per-period triangle to an annual triangle — create_annual_triangle
text: cumulative annual triangle (numeric matrix).

title: Re-aggregate a per-period triangle to an annual triangle — create_annual_triangle
code: # 4x4 quarterly cumulative triangle (only the lower-left corner is real data) q_trg <- matrix(NA_real_, 4, 4) q_trg[1, ] <- c(1, 2, 3, 4) q_trg[2, 1:3] <- c(2, 4, 5) q_trg[3, 1:2] <- c(3, 5) q_trg[4, 1]   <- 4 create_annual_triangle(q_trg, period = 4) #>      [,1] #> [1,]   18

what: Average development factors from a CHL factor triangle — create_avg_coefs
title: Average development factors from a CHL factor triangle — create_avg_coefs
text: Averages per-cell development factors CHL factor triangle along diagonals. requested window length, returns one row simple-average factor per development period.

title: Average development factors from a CHL factor triangle — create_avg_coefs
Average development factors from a CHL factor triangle — create_avg_coefs
text: chl_trg development-factor triangle (e.g. output create_chl_coef_triangle()). avg_length Vector – element either "full" (use full diagonal) integer giving window length.

title: Average development factors from a CHL factor triangle — create_avg_coefs
text: data.frame one row per avg_length element.

what: Element-wise chain-ladder development factor triangle — create_chl_coef_triangle
title: Element-wise chain-ladder development factor triangle — create_chl_coef_triangle
text: every cell (, j) cumulative triangle returns cum_trg[, j] / cum_trg[, j-1]. first column upper-right NA corner left NA.

title: Element-wise chain-ladder development factor triangle — create_chl_coef_triangle
Element-wise chain-ladder development factor triangle — create_chl_coef_triangle
text: cum_trg cumulative numeric triangle.

title: Element-wise chain-ladder development factor triangle — create_chl_coef_triangle

title: Element-wise chain-ladder development factor triangle — create_chl_coef_triangle
code: cum <- matrix(c(10, 30, 32,  20, 27, NA,  15, NA, NA), nrow = 3, byrow = TRUE) create_chl_coef_triangle(cum) #>      [,1] [,2]     [,3] #> [1,]   NA 3.00 1.066667 #> [2,]   NA 1.35       NA #> [3,]   NA   NA       NA

title: Volume-weighted chain-ladder development factors — create_chl_coefs
text: Computes one row development factors per requested length ("full" integer giving many recent rows use). Factors aggregated using standard volume-weighted formula sum(cum[, j]) / sum(cum[, j - 1]).

title: Volume-weighted chain-ladder development factors — create_chl_coefs
Volume-weighted chain-ladder development factors — create_chl_coefs
text: cum_trg cumulative numeric triangle. chl_length vector – element either literal "full" (use rows) numeric/integer giving window length. Default "full".

title: Volume-weighted chain-ladder development factors — create_chl_coefs
text: data.frame one row per chl_length element, first column labelling row remaining columns holding development factors.

title: Volume-weighted chain-ladder development factors — create_chl_coefs
code: cum <- matrix(c(10, 30, 32,  20, 27, NA,  15, NA, NA), nrow = 3, byrow = TRUE) create_chl_coefs(cum, chl_length = c("full", 2)) #>          CH_L_lengths  0   1        2 #> 1 chain_ladder - full NA 1.9 1.066667 #> 2    chain_ladder - 2 NA 1.9 1.066667

what: Add zero/value columns based on equality conditions — create_find_columns
title: Add zero/value columns based on equality conditions — create_find_columns
text: pair (condition_names[], new_cols_names[]) function adds new column data initialised 0. row matches data[[condition_var]] == condition_names[], new column set value data[[values_var]] (evaluated expression - see Details).

title: Add zero/value columns based on equality conditions — create_find_columns
code: create_find_columns(   data,   condition_names,   new_cols_names,   condition_var,   values_var )

title: Add zero/value columns based on equality conditions — create_find_columns
text: data data.frame data.table. condition_names Character vector values match condition_var. new_cols_names Character vector names new columns create. length condition_names. condition_var Character scalar - name column test. values_var Character scalar - expression yields value assign condition matches.

title: Add zero/value columns based on equality conditions — create_find_columns
text: updated data.table (modified place).

title: Add zero/value columns based on equality conditions — create_find_columns
text: values_var argument parsed evaluated inside data[, ...], means can bare column name small data.table-style expression. function modifies data reference (via data.table::setDT()) also returns invisibly allow chaining.

title: Add zero/value columns based on equality conditions — create_find_columns
code: dt <- data.frame(line = c("MTPL", "CASCO", "MTPL"), premium = c(100, 200, 300)) create_find_columns(   data = dt,   condition_names = c("MTPL", "CASCO"),   new_cols_names = c("MTPL_premium", "CASCO_premium"),   condition_var = "line",   values_var = "premium" ) #> Index: <line> #>      line premium MTPL_premium CASCO_premium #>    <char>   <num>        <num>         <num> #> 1:   MTPL     100          100             0 #> 2:  CASCO     200            0           200 #> 3:   MTPL     300          300             0

code: df <- data.frame(exp_q1 = c(0.25, 0.5), exp_q2 = c(0.5, 0.5), premium = c(100, 200)) create_multiple_columns_m(   data               = df,   new_variable_names = c("prem_q1", "prem_q2"),   multiple_exp_names = c("exp_q1", "exp_q2"),   multiply_var       = "premium" ) #>   exp_q1 exp_q2 premium prem_q1 prem_q2 #> 1   0.25    0.5     100      25      50 #> 2   0.50    0.5     200     100     100

text: Given one-row data frame development factors returned create_chl_coefs(), computes cumulative product development period tail.

title: Cumulative product of chain-ladder factors (ultimate development) — create_product_coefs
Cumulative product of chain-ladder factors (ultimate development) — create_product_coefs
text: chl_coefs one-row data frame whose first column label whose columns hold development factors. (returned create_chl_coefs().) name Character – label resulting row.

title: Cumulative product of chain-ladder factors (ultimate development) — create_product_coefs
text: character vector cumulative-product factors name first element.

what: Run-off check across all diagonals — create_run_off_check
title: Run-off check across all diagonals — create_run_off_check
text: Compares diagonal's lower-tail cumulative ultimate main diagonal's. Returns run-differences chronological order.

title: Run-off check across all diagonals — create_run_off_check
Run-off check across all diagonals — create_run_off_check
text: cum_trg cumulative numeric triangle.

title: Run-off check across all diagonals — create_run_off_check
text: Numeric vector run-differences length = nrow(cum_trg) - 1.

what: Build a development triangle from row-level claims data — create_triangle
title: Build a development triangle from row-level claims data — create_triangle
text: Aggregates value (row_num, col_num) (optionally filtering one cond_variable == cond_value pairs) arranges result square rows x rows matrix upper-right corner beyond main anti-diagonal NA.

title: Build a development triangle from row-level claims data — create_triangle
code: create_triangle(   data,   row_num,   col_num,   value,   cond_variable = NULL,   cond_value = NULL,   rows = NULL )

title: Build a development triangle from row-level claims data — create_triangle
text: data data frame (tibble) least columns named row_num, col_num value. row_num Character - name column row indices (origin periods, 1-based). col_num Character - name column column indices (development periods, 0-based). value Character - name numeric column summed. cond_variable Optional character vector - names columns filter . cond_value Optional vector values, length cond_variable - equality filter applied pairwise. rows Optional integer - size output triangle. Defaults maximum value data[[row_num]].

title: Build a development triangle from row-level claims data — create_triangle
text: numeric rows x rows matrix.

title: Build a development triangle from row-level claims data — create_triangle
code: df <- expand.grid(origin = 1:3, dev = 0:2) df$paid <- c(100, 50, 20,  120, 40, NA,  130, NA, NA) df <- df[!is.na(df$paid), ] create_triangle(df, row_num = "origin", col_num = "dev", value = "paid") #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] #> [1,]  100  120  130 #> [2,]   50   40   NA #> [3,]   20   NA   NA

what: Read values along a triangle diagonal — diag_reader
title: Read values along a triangle diagonal — diag_reader
text: Returns values along diag_num-th anti-diagonal trg, starting bottom-left.

title: Read values along a triangle diagonal — diag_reader
Read values along a triangle diagonal — diag_reader
text: trg square numeric matrix (triangle). diag_num Integer - anti-diagonal read. Defaults main (last) one.

title: Read values along a triangle diagonal — diag_reader
text: numeric vector values diagonal.

title: Read values along a triangle diagonal — diag_reader
code: trg <- matrix(1:9, nrow = 3) diag_reader(trg) #> [1] 3 5 7 diag_reader(trg, diag_num = 2) #> [1] 2 4

what: Cumulative diagonal sums of a triangle — diag_sums
title: Cumulative diagonal sums of a triangle — diag_sums
text: anti-diagonal k = 1..nrow(trg) returns sum values.

title: Cumulative diagonal sums of a triangle — diag_sums
Cumulative diagonal sums of a triangle — diag_sums
text: trg square numeric matrix (triangle).

title: Cumulative diagonal sums of a triangle — diag_sums
text: Numeric vector length nrow(trg).

title: Cumulative diagonal sums of a triangle — diag_sums
code: diag_sums(matrix(c(10, 5, 2,  20, 7, NA,  15, NA, NA), nrow = 3, byrow = TRUE)) #> [1] 10 25 24

what: Append the latest diagonal of a new triangle to an old one — diag_writer
title: Append the latest diagonal of a new triangle to an old one — diag_writer
text: Used rolling re-runs: pads old_trg one extra row column NA, fills gaps new_trg (already contains newly observed diagonal).

title: Append the latest diagonal of a new triangle to an old one — diag_writer
Append the latest diagonal of a new triangle to an old one — diag_writer
text: old_trg (n) x (n) matrix - old triangle. new_trg (n+1) x (n+1) matrix - new triangle including freshly observed diagonal.

title: Append the latest diagonal of a new triangle to an old one — diag_writer
text: merged (n+1) x (n+1) matrix.

title: Append the latest diagonal of a new triangle to an old one — diag_writer
code: old <- matrix(c(10, 5, 20, NA), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE) new <- matrix(c(10, 5, 3, 20, 6, NA, 25, NA, NA), nrow = 3, byrow = TRUE) diag_writer(old, new) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] #> [1,]   10    5    3 #> [2,]   20    6   NA #> [3,]   25   NA   NA

what: Pick n evenly spaced colors from a rainbow data frame — get_colors
title: Pick n evenly spaced colors from a rainbow data frame — get_colors
text: Three flavours exported. differ post-processing apply picking equally-spaced rows rgb_df:

title: Pick n evenly spaced colors from a rainbow data frame — get_colors
code: get_colors(rgb_df, n_colors)  get_colors_plus(rgb_df, n_colors)  get_colors_duo(rgb_df, n_colors)

title: Pick n evenly spaced colors from a rainbow data frame — get_colors
text: rgb_df data frame columns r, g, b, e.g. produced rgb_colors_for_plot(). n_colors Integer >= 2 – number colors return.

title: Pick n evenly spaced colors from a rainbow data frame — get_colors
text: character vector length n_colors hex color codes ("#RRGGBB").

title: Pick n evenly spaced colors from a rainbow data frame — get_colors
text: get_colors() – post-processing. get_colors_plus() – every color darkened (x0.75) lightened (towards 255 weight 0.7), creating strong duo pattern. get_colors_duo() – every color darkened (x0.8) lightened (weight 0.6), milder _plus variant.

title: Pick n evenly spaced colors from a rainbow data frame — get_colors

what: Convert a Czech birth number to a date of birth — rc_to_birth_day
title: Convert a Czech birth number to a date of birth — rc_to_birth_day
text: three variants implement slightly different rules resolving century (two-digit year stored Czech rodne cislo). kept separate functions existing code depends particular rule keeps working.

title: Convert a Czech birth number to a date of birth — rc_to_birth_day
Convert a Czech birth number to a date of birth — rc_to_birth_day
text: rc_s_lomitkem Character vector. Czech birth number, optionally / separating suffix. first six digits (YYMMDD) matters date.

title: Convert a Czech birth number to a date of birth — rc_to_birth_day
text: Date vector length rc_s_lomitkem.

title: Convert a Czech birth number to a date of birth — rc_to_birth_day
text: rc_to_birth_day() - assumes input trailing 4-digit suffix separated slash ("YYMMDD/XXXX", total length 11). Years < 54 go 21st century, otherwise 20th. rc_to_birth_day_2() - check length uses cut-25 - anything < 25 21st century, otherwise 20th. rc_to_birth_day_3() - like rc_to_birth_day() cut-25. three apply women-offset (+50 added month women, months 51-62 -> 01-12).

title: Convert a Czech birth number to a date of birth — rc_to_birth_day

what: vitopack: Actuarial Helpers for Triangles, Exposures and Czech Birth Numbers — vitopack-package
title: vitopack: Actuarial Helpers for Triangles, Exposures and Czech Birth Numbers — vitopack-package
text: collection utilities grew day--day non-life actuarial work Com-PASS Advisory. package split thematic families:

title: vitopack: Actuarial Helpers for Triangles, Exposures and Czech Birth Numbers — vitopack-package
text: Triangles & chain-ladder - create_triangle(), create_cumulative_triangle(), create_chl_coefs(), create_chl_coefs_weighted(), create_run_off_check() friends. Policy columns & exposures - create_find_columns(), create_policy_exposure_columns_m(), create_policy_exposure_days_columns(), create_multiple_columns_m(). Czech birth numbers - rc_to_birth_day(), rc_to_birth_day_2(), rc_to_birth_day_3(). Color palettes - rgb_colors_for_plot(), get_colors(), get_colors_plus(), get_colors_duo(), plot_color_bars(), rainbow_cat(). /O & misc - load_excel_sheets(), load_xlsb_sheets(), numeric_format(), bind_with_source().

title: vitopack: Actuarial Helpers for Triangles, Exposures and Czech Birth Numbers — vitopack-package
text: Maintainer: Ondřej Vít ondrej.vit@com-pass.cz

title: vitopack 0.1.1
text: Added reference chain-ladder methodology DESCRIPTION field: Mack (1993) doi:10.2143/AST.23.2.2005092. Removed (interactive()) wrapper plot_color_bars() example – function works non-interactive sessions .

title: vitopack 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Triangles & chain-ladder helpers: create_triangle(), create_cumulative_triangle(), create_decumulative_triangle(), create_annual_triangle(), create_run_off_check(), diag_reader(), diag_sums(), diag_writer(), triangle_namer(). Development factors: create_chl_coef_triangle(), create_chl_coefs(), create_chl_coefs_weighted(), create_chl_coefs_weighted_future(), create_avg_coefs(), create_product_coefs(). Plotly visualisations: create_chl_trg_visualization(), create_chl_trg_visualization_pvzp(). Policy column helpers: create_find_columns(), create_policy_exposure_columns_m(), create_policy_exposure_days_columns(), create_multiple_columns_m(), bind_with_source(). Czech birth-number parsers: rc_to_birth_day(), rc_to_birth_day_2(), rc_to_birth_day_3(). Color palette helpers: rgb_colors_for_plot(), get_colors(), get_colors_plus(), get_colors_duo(), plot_color_bars(), rainbow_cat(). /O helpers: load_excel_sheets(), load_xlsb_sheets(), numeric_format().
